economy.commentary:
Federal Reserve Posted Loss of $18.7 Billion in 2025: The central bank's finances are recovering after an unprecedented run of losses tied to its pandemic-era stimulus and subsequent inflation fight.

Trump says Iran wants deal but is 'afraid to say it': President Donald Trump said Iranian leaders are negotiating with the United States in private to end the war but do not want to say so publicly because they fear for their lives.

crypto.commentary: Crypto exchange Binance has lost more than half its market share, now controlling 27% of Bitcoin trading.

real_estate.homebuilder: President Trump recently signed Executive Order 14394, "Removing Regulatory Barriers to Affordable Home Construction," which directs federal agencies to streamline environmental permitting and reduce regulatory burdens for residential developers.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area market remains a "seller's market" due to severely constrained inventory, though it is becoming more localized; while San Francisco supply is extremely tight at 0.8 months, Santa Clara County has seen a 21% increase in days on market compared to last year, signaling slightly more buyer selectivity.

📍 Latest Real Estate News in the Bay Area: As of late March 2026, the Bay Area housing market is described as "split," with single-family homes remaining highly competitive while the condo market offers more flexibility for buyers. In Santa Clara County, the median home price has stabilized at approximately $2M, and while inventory remains low (1.1 months of supply), homes are staying on the market longer than in previous cycles. In Alameda County, including Fremont, buyer caution is being offset by improved affordability from rates that have dipped from their 2025 peaks, though national average rates have ticked up slightly this week due to inflation concerns tied to Middle East energy costs.

📍 Latest General News in the Bay Area: In Milpitas, the community recently celebrated the grand opening of the new Milpitas Performing Arts Center, a state-of-the-art facility that officially opened with a ribbon-cutting on March 20. Local government in Milpitas has also seen a leadership shift with the hiring of Madison Nguyen as the city's new Interim Economic Development Director. In Fremont, the focus remains on regional transit and safety; BART recently issued advisories for late-night maintenance through the Transbay Tube, and residents are tracking the impact of new federal housing executive orders aimed at reducing local development costs.
